Title: Genomic and genetic insights into Mendel's pea genes

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCLE41 was identified as the strongest candidate for the P locus controlling parchmentless pod phenotype. 2. An in-frame premature stop codon (R79*) upstream of the TDIF motif fully explains the p phenotype. 3. The findings support a model in which PsCLE41 controls pod sclerenchyma development, lignification and parchment formation through TDIF-PXY-WOX signalling.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed exclusively in pod; sampled in pod tissues during pod development.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PsCLE41 function reduces sclerenchyma development and lignification, causing parchmentless edible pods.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pod developmental stage; pod samples collected after flowering under field/greenhouse growth conditions.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PXY-like protein; PsPXY; PsWOX4; PsWOX14; PsMYB26; PsNAC
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; F2 mapping; haplotype-phenotype association; expression analysis by RNA-seq and RT-qPCR; anatomical analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal allele carries an Arg79* premature stop codon upstream of the TDIF motif. The paper presents PsCLE41 as a signalling peptide controlling cell fate and lignification rather than a classical enzyme or TF, so it is classified as Common Gene.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides pod sclerenchyma/parchment through lignification, influencing edible-pod market type and pod texture.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding edible-pod, parchmentless pea types via selection of the p allele.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375; RNA-seq: PRJCA023166/CRA014669/CRA023374/CRA023375

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCIK2/3 was identified as the Fa candidate gene for fasciated stem phenotype. 2. A 5-bp deletion in exon 2 causes a frameshift and premature stop codon associated with fa lines. 3. PsCIK2/3 interacts with PsCLV1 and PsCLV2 and likely functions in CLV3-WUS meristem homeostasis. 4. Fasciation is modified by a second locus, mfa.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in stem; also detected in apical bud; significantly downregulated in fa mutant apical bud and stem.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PsCIK2/3 function causes fasciated stems with broadened apical meristem and clustered flowers.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage of apical bud and stem; field and greenhouse growth conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; bulked segregant analysis; fine mapping; haplotype analysis; RT-qPCR; in situ hybridization; subcellular localization; yeast two-hybrid
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CLAVATA3 INSENSITIVE RECEPTOR KINASE-like co-receptor kinase; senescence-associated receptor-like kinase
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A modifier locus, mfa, masks fasciation in some fa/fa backgrounds. PsCIK2/3 controls meristem structure, not a nutrient metabolite, but is a core experimentally studied gene in the paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to manipulate plant architecture, but potential complexity due to modifier locus mfa.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375; RNA-seq: PRJCA023166/CRA014669/CRA023374/CRA023375

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsOs1 was identified as the main candidate gene at the organ size locus affecting pod width and hundred-seed weight. 2. PsOs1 encodes a SIAMESE-related cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor likely affecting cell division and enlargement. 3. VIGS and transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis supported a causal role in seed weight and pod width regulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most highly expressed gene within the fine-mapped interval; expression assessed in pod tissues and developmental stages.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates larger organ size, increasing pod width and seed weight.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pod development stage; greenhouse and field growth conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; fine mapping with KASP markers; differential expression analysis; VIGS; transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis; flow cytometry; promoter activity assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is an agronomic trait gene affecting organ size rather than a direct metabolic enzyme/regulator of a defined nutrient metabolite, but it influences seed size and weight relevant to nutritional yield.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve pea seed weight and pod width.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375; RNA-seq: PRJCA023166/CRA014669/CRA023374/CRA023375; WGBS reported in study
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: DNA methylation sequencing was performed on JI0074 reference context, but no explicit causal epigenetic regulation of PsOs1 was stated.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters from JI0074 and JI1995 compared in GUS assays

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal esterification step of chlorophyll synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant; key functional step for green tissue pigmentation, not explicitly stated as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gp was shown to correspond to ChlG. 2. An approximately 100-kb deletion upstream of ChlG in gp lines generates aberrant intergenic fusion transcripts with a truncated NLR gene, reducing functional ChlG transcript abundance to about 6% of wild type. 3. The resulting ChlG deficiency disrupts chlorophyll biosynthesis and causes yellow pods and other yellow-green tissues. 4. A ChlG W121* TILLING allele failed to complement gp, confirming gene identity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional ChlG transcript reduced in gp pods; intact transcript relatively more abundant in leaves than pods; expressed in green tissues including pod and leaf.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pods, leaves, petioles, rachis, tendrils, leaflet midribs, pedicel, peduncle and sepals
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll accumulation; ChlG deficiency causes yellow pod and impaired chloroplast/thylakoid development.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Green versus yellow pod phenotyping in mature flowering and fruiting plants; tissue-specific effects in pods and leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; genetic mapping; RNA-seq; RT-PCR/cDNA sequencing of fusion transcripts; qPCR; TILLING mutant complementation test; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal natural gp allele is not a coding mutation in ChlG itself but an adjacent large deletion causing transcriptional interference and aberrant fused transcripts. Homozygous ChlG W121* is embryo lethal.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigment required for green coloration and chloroplast function.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid deleterious gp/ChlG deficiency in breeding unless yellow pod phenotype is specifically desired; caution because strong loss of function can be embryo lethal.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced productivity and disturbed thylakoid development in gp lines; null allele homozygotes embryo lethal.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375; RNA-seq: PRJCA023166/CRA014669/CRA023374/CRA023375
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: JI0015; JI2366; JI2822; Caméor; TILLING line 411.1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter/genic context disrupted by upstream deletion

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with retention of target product; initiates chlorophyll breakdown affecting cotyledon color
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first step of chlorophyll degradation according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The I locus corresponds to the Mg-dechelatase gene controlling yellow versus green cotyledons. 2. The study characterized two key mutant alleles, i-1 and i-2, involving a retrotransposon insertion and a promoter deletion. 3. Transcriptional differences at this gene contribute to chlorophyll retention/degeneration phenotypes in cotyledons.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression differences associated with promoter/transposon alleles in cotyledons.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll degradation; reduced/altered function causes green cotyledons.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation and environmental factors influence penetrance of cotyledon color phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; allele characterization; promoter/transposon analysis; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a core Mendelian metabolism gene discussed in results, though this paper mainly adds novel allelic information rather than first functional discovery.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines cotyledon green/yellow coloration through chlorophyll turnover.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for seed appearance and stay-green traits.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Phenotypic variability depends on transcriptional and environmental factors.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter; i-2 carries 408-bp promoter deletion

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target storage carbohydrate architecture
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for wrinkled versus round seed phenotype; not explicitly termed rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The R locus signal mapped to the expected position of PsSBE. 2. The paper presents the full-length sequence of the Ips-r transposon insertion in the last exon. 3. R versus r is also the primary determinant of seed protein content variation, likely through altered starch quality and quantity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal starch branching and round seeds; mutant r causes wrinkled seeds and indirectly affects seed protein content.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The manuscript emphasizes allelic diversity and nutritional association with seed protein content.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage carbohydrate affecting seed texture and nutritional composition.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modifying starch quality and indirectly seed protein composition.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered starch may shift storage protein class ratios and overall protein content.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for active GA production in this trait context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The Le gene was confirmed as GA3ox1. 2. Five haplotypes were observed, but reduced-height le variants were exclusively associated with haplotype 1 carrying the known G>A substitution. 3. This validates molecular diversity at the Mendelian dwarfing locus.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes bioactive GA production and tall growth; le reduces height.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient metabolite gene, but a core Mendelian pathway gene in results.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary cell wall thickening and lignification in pod endocarp
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of PsNAC and lignification-associated genes; reduced PsMYB26 expression correlates with reduced PsNAC expression.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Likely developmental pod endocarp program; genetically influenced by PsCLE41/P signaling and upstream Ogre insertion allele
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsMYB26 is the strongest candidate for the V locus controlling parchmentless pods. 2. All v/v accessions are associated with a 23-kb Ogre-type LTR retrotransposon insertion upstream of PsMYB26. 3. PsMYB26 is specifically expressed in pod endocarp and is strongly downregulated in v/v lines. 4. VIGS and RT-qPCR showed that reduced PsMYB26 lowers PsNAC expression, supporting its role in secondary wall thickening and lignification.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed specifically in pod, particularly pod endocarp; highly differentially expressed between wild type and v/v mutant pods.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates lignification and parchment formation; reduced expression causes parchmentless edible pods.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; F2 mapping; haplotype association; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; VIGS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major switch for pod sclerenchyma secondary wall thickening and lignification at the V locus.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PsMYB26 and the associated Ogre insertion are strong candidates for V, the authors state that further work is needed to fully elucidate the detailed molecular mechanism of the v allele.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides lignified parchment layer, determining edible versus shelling pod type.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for breeding edible-pod pea by selecting or engineering reduced PsMYB26 activity.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced lignification may affect pod strength or dehiscence-related traits.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375; RNA-seq: PRJCA023166/CRA014669/CRA023374/CRA023375
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter region; v-associated 23-kb upstream Ogre insertion

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin pathway genes; participates with D-locus MYBs and A2 WD40 in pigmentation complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes anthocyanin pigmentation throughout the plant.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Part of MYB(D)-bHLH(A)-WD40(A2) complex proposed for pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The A gene was confirmed and several novel alleles were identified. 2. A rare intragenic suppressor mutation restores the common white-flower a allele to a functional A allele. 3. A controls anthocyanin pigmentation and is epistatic to D for axil ring pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers, axil rings and pigmented tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation; loss causes white flowers and loss of pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phenotyped in field environments including Harbin for axil ring pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of anthocyanin pigmentation across tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core Mendelian regulatory gene mainly included for allelic discovery. Not a nutritional metabolite focus but directly regulates a specialized metabolite.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for visible marker traits and anthocyanin manipulation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin pigmentation patterning in axil ring
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Requires colored flower background; influenced by D locus context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely part of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with A and A2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VIGS of PsMYB16 reduced axil ring anthocyanin pigmentation. 2. PsMYB16 is one candidate MYB within the D locus cluster regulating axil ring pigmentation. 3. The D locus is genetically complex and likely involves multiple MYB genes.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed in colored-flower lines; field data from Harbin 2022.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to D-locus control of axil ring pigmentation but not alone sufficient to explain all allelic complexity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper concludes D is genetically complex and may depend on combinations of multiple MYB genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be used to modulate pigmentation patterns.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin pigmentation patterning in axil ring
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes axil ring anthocyanin accumulation as part of D locus cluster.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely part of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with A and A2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fast neutron deletion of PsMYB104 together with PsMYB106 abolished axil ring pigmentation. 2. The deletion was allelic to d, implicating these MYB genes in D-locus function.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal axil ring anthocyanin pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; fast neutron deletion analysis; allelism tests
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to complex D-locus determination of axil ring pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact contribution relative to neighboring MYBs remains unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin pigmentation patterning in axil ring
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes axil ring anthocyanin accumulation as part of D locus cluster.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely part of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with A and A2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fast neutron deletion of PsMYB106 together with PsMYB104 abolished axil ring pigmentation. 2. This deletion is allelic to d, implicating these genes at the D locus.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal axil ring anthocyanin pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; fast neutron deletion analysis; allelism tests
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to complex D-locus determination of axil ring pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: D locus behavior suggests combined alleles of multiple MYB genes determine pigmentation pattern.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing: BioProject PRJCA023166; GSA CRA014669, CRA023374, CRA023375
